title: oxytropis viscidula
intro: Oxytropis viscidula, also known as sticky milkvetch, is a plant species in the pea family Fabaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, which help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, providing potential health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Oxytropis viscidula is often used as a dried root or extracted into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
